the diagram models a system architecture with components for authentication, user profile management, data management, report generation, notification, and achievement tracking.
the datamanager component is central, providing data to the reporthandler and accessing the database.
the reporthandler displays reports via the webserver.
the notifysystem is responsible for notifying users about achievements.

this diagram models a system for managing reports, user accounts, consumption data, and game scores.
users create reports that record consumption data, which is then used to generate suggestions and manage objectives.
the system tracks green points earned through consumption and updates game scores.

the diagram models the process of creating a user.
the main component initiates the process by requesting user data from the local user.
the data manager checks the data and, if necessary, prompts the user to re-enter it.
finally, the data manager adds the user and signals success back to the main component.

the diagram models a sequence of interactions between components to authenticate a user.
the flow starts with the main component initiating the authentication process.
the authentication process involves checking the user's credentials and retrieving user data from the datamanager.
the success of the authentication is then passed back to the main component.

main receives the `checkconsumi` message from the local user.
the local user sends a `report` request to the main.
the main sends a `new_data` request to the data manager.
the data manager updates the report and sends a `success` message back to the main.

the user initiates the process by sending a report.
the main component then interacts with the data manager to update the report.
the success of the update is indicated by a "success" message.
